growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: microbe-directory.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: acidic mineral (mineral salts) medium with methane/methanol as carbon/energy source

up.method: reported (qualitative: group 1h [NiFe] hydrogenase supported growth on H2)

growth_records.cite: Schmitz (review; specific data from Mohammadi et al. 2017a as cited in text) et al. Verrucomicrobial methanotrophs: ecophysiology of metabolically versatile acidophiles. FEMS Microbiology Reviews (2021). DOI:10.1093/femsre/fuab007. [PMC8498564]
growth_records.snippet: At dissolved oxygen concentrations between 0.2 and 1.5%, only the group 1h [NiFe] hydrogenase supported growth, up to a growth rate of 0.03 h-1, which is almost 40% of the growth rate on methane (Mohammadi et al. 2017a)
growth_records.notes: Number (0.03 h-1) explicit in this review paper's text but originates from Mohammadi et al. 2017a (cited primary study), reported here for M. fumariolicum SolV growing on H2 at dissolved O2 0.2-1.5%. pH(2)/T(55C) taken from this paper's Table 1 species-optimum summary, not restated alongside the specific rate sentence. A second value (growth rate on methane) can be back-calculated: 0.03/0.40 = 0.075 h-1 (calculated; input 'almost 40%' is itself approximate, so this derived number is low-confidence and not included as a separate record). [metadata completed from paper Methods: medium]

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium (pH 2.7), ammonium N source

up.method: calculated from reported 280 nmol O2/min/mg DW: 280 x 60 / 1000 = 16.8 mmol/gDW/h
calculated from reported 15-20 nmol O2/min/mg DW (~6% of methane rate): midpoint 17.5 x 60/1000 = 1.05 mmol/gDW/h (range 0.9-1.2)
not directly reported; O2 respiration with methane = 280 nmol O2/min/mg DW (16.8 mmol/gDW/h)

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: Cells from batch cultures of strain SolV growing on methane at maximum growth rate (mu=0.07 h-1) and oxygen concentrations above 10% showed relatively high oxygen consumption rates at the expense of hydrogen, 15-20 nmol.min-1.mg DW-1 ... ab
growth_records.notes: Batch on methane, mu_max reported = 0.07 h-1 (O2 >10%). td = ln2/0.07 = 9.90 h. qO2 conversions: 1 nmol/min/mg DW = 60/1000 mmol/gDW/h = 0.06 mmol/gDW/h. Methane O2 respiration 280 nmol/min/mg DW = 16.8 mmol/gDW/h; hydrogen-driven O2 respiration 15-20 nmol/min/mg DW = 0.9-1.2 (mid 1.05) mmol/gDW/h. Growth on H2+CO2 not possible at this high O2. 55C, 250 rpm, pH 2.7, headspace 10% CH4 / 5% CO2 in air.

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium (pH 2.7), ammonium N source
growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: A batch culture growing at mu_max (0.075 h-1) in a bioreactor on methane at low oxygen concentration (regulated at 0.2% oxygen by mass flow controllers) also showed increased hydrogen oxidizing activity.
growth_records.notes: Batch bioreactor on methane at 0.2% O2; mu_max reported = 0.075 h-1; td = ln2/0.075 = 9.24 h. On switching methane->hydrogen, O2 consumption dropped and stabilized at 13% of the methane O2 consumption rate; 6% biomass increase (OD600 0.85->0.9) over 3 h confirms continued growth on H2/CO2. Microaerophilic (0.2% O2).

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium

up.method: calculated from reported 268-317 nmol O2/min/mg DW: midpoint 292.5 x 60/1000 = 17.55 mmol/gDW/h (range 16.08-19.02)
calculated from reported 20-24 nmol O2/min/mg DW: midpoint 22 x 60/1000 = 1.32 mmol/gDW/h (range 1.2-1.44)

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: The hydrogen respiration rate of a continuous culture under methane limitation (D=0.03 h-1) ... 20-24 nmol O2.min-1.mg DW-1 ... being about 7.5% of the methane respiration rates (268-317 nmol O2.min-1.mg DW-1).
growth_records.notes: Methane-limited chemostat, D=0.03 h-1 so mu=0.03; td=ln2/0.03=23.1 h. qO2 (methane) 268-317 nmol/min/mg DW = 16.08-19.02 mmol/gDW/h (mid 17.55). qO2 (hydrogen, via constitutive hhy-type hydrogenase) 20-24 nmol/min/mg DW = 1.2-1.44 mmol/gDW/h (mid 1.32); ~7.5% of methane rate. Rates unaffected by dO2 0.3-3.2%, indicating O2-insensitive hydrogenase.

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium

up.method: reported qualitatively as at least twice the methane-limited value (i.e. >=2.4-2.9 mmol/gDW/h vs 1.2-1.44 at D=0.03); no single value given due to inactivation/reactivation events

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: When a methane-fed continuous culture was grown under oxygen limitation (D=0.015 h-1), hydrogen respiration measurements became less straightforward with inactivation and reactivation events occurring ... Hydrogen respiration rates measured
growth_records.notes: O2-limited methane chemostat, D=0.015 h-1 so mu=0.015; td=ln2/0.015=46.2 h. Under O2 limitation the O2-sensitive hup-type hydrogenase is additionally expressed; H2 respiration >= 2x the D=0.03 methane-limited value (which was 1.2-1.44 mmol/gDW/h), so >= ~2.4-2.9 mmol/gDW/h, but no precise single value given. Microaerophilic/O2-limited.

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium (pH 2.7)

up.method: calculated from reported 220 nmol H2/min/mg DW: 220 x 60/1000 = 13.2 mmol/gDW/h (bottle incubation, 0.5% H2, 0.1-0.2% O2)
calculated from reported 66-71 nmol O2/min/mg DW (O2 <5 uM): midpoint 68.5 x 60/1000 = 4.11 mmol/gDW/h (range 3.96-4.26)

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: Cells sampled from the batch cultures growing on hydrogen at the highest growth rate obtained (mu=0.047 h-1) ... respiration rates with hydrogen measured were as high as 66-71 nmol O2.min-1.mg DW-1 ... hydrogen consumption rate ... was 220
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic 'Knallgas' batch growth on H2/CO2, highest growth rate mu=0.047 h-1 (=60% of the methane rate; at O2 <0.2%). td=ln2/0.047=14.75 h. qH2 = 220 nmol/min/mg DW x 60/1000 = 13.2 mmol/gDW/h. qO2 (H2-driven) = 66-71 nmol/min/mg DW = 3.96-4.26 mmol/gDW/h (mid 4.11). Reported O2:H2 consumption ratio 0.32 is consistent: 4.11/13.2 = 0.31. Sole carbon source is CO2. Microaerophilic.

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium

up.method: calculated: qH2 = mu / Yx/s = 0.026 h-1 / 3.4 g DW per mol H2 = 0.007647 mol H2/gDW/h = 7.65 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: reported: 8-10% of total organic matter found in supernatant (excreted), comparable to methane growth; no specific rate

yields.Yxs: 3.4 +/- 0.1 g DW per mol H2 (biomass yield on hydrogen)

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: An oxygen-limited continuous culture on hydrogen (D=0.026 h-1) was used to assess the growth yield by accurately measuring the hydrogen consumption. Based on dry weight (DW) measurements a yield value of 3.4+/-0.1 g DW.mole H2-1 was obtaine
growth_records.notes: O2-limited H2 chemostat, D=0.026 h-1 so mu=0.026; td=ln2/0.026=26.66 h. Reported biomass yield Yx/s = 3.4 +/- 0.1 g DW per mol H2. Specific H2 uptake derived: qH2 = mu/Yxs = 0.026/3.4 = 0.0076 mol/gDW/h = 7.65 mmol/gDW/h. 8-10% of total organic matter excreted to supernatant (no rate). Microaerophilic.

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium

up.method: calculated from reported Vmax 127 nmol H2/min/mg DW: 127 x 60/1000 = 7.62 mmol/gDW/h; apparent Ks(H2)=0.6 uM

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: The kinetics for the oxygen-insensitive hydrogenase were more accurately assessed in cells grown on hydrogen in batch at dO2 value at 0.8% (D=0.02 h-1) ... an apparent hydrogen Ks of 0.6 uM was obtained (measured Vmax=127 nmol H2.min-1.mg D
growth_records.notes: Batch growth on H2 at dO2 0.8%; paper labels growth rate as D=0.02 h-1 so mu=0.02; td=ln2/0.02=34.66 h. Hydrogen-uptake Michaelis-Menten: Vmax=127 nmol H2/min/mg DW = 7.62 mmol/gDW/h, apparent Ks(H2)=0.6 uM (oxygen-insensitive hhy-type hydrogenase). Microaerophilic (0.8% O2).

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium

up.method: calculated from reported Vmax 159 nmol H2/min/mg DW: 159 x 60/1000 = 9.54 mmol/gDW/h; apparent Ks(H2)=1.1 uM

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: Hydrogenase kinetics of cells growing at lower dO2 of 0.2%, conditions (D=0.03 h-1) in which additional expression of the oxygen-sensitive hydrogenase is expected, showed a higher hydrogen consumption rate (Vmax=159 nmol H2.min-1.mg DW-1) b
growth_records.notes: Continuous culture on H2 at dO2 0.2%, D=0.03 h-1 so mu=0.03; td=ln2/0.03=23.1 h. Mixed hydrogenase activity (hup + hhy): Vmax(H2)=159 nmol/min/mg DW = 9.54 mmol/gDW/h, apparent Ks(H2)=1.1 uM. Oxygen-sensitive hup-type contributes ~2x the O2-insensitive rate; its apparent Ks estimated 1-2 uM. Microaerophilic (0.2% O2).

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium

up.method: reported qualitatively; methane respiration rate much higher than hydrogen; drops below 15 uM CH4 (Ks methane 6 uM); simultaneous consumption of methane and hydrogen
reported qualitatively; H2 consumed simultaneously with methane; rate constant until H2 fully consumed (Ks well below 1 uM)

growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: Addition of hydrogen to a methane-limited culture (D=0.013 h-1, at dO2 0.5-1%) resulted in an immediate increase of biomass (DW) and both substrates were consumed simultaneously.
growth_records.notes: Methane-limited chemostat with H2 co-fed, D=0.013 h-1 so mu=0.013; td=ln2/0.013=53.3 h. Methane and hydrogen consumed simultaneously; hydrogen boosts biomass. Methane Ks 6 uM (from Pol et al 2007); H2 respiration constant until H2 exhausted (Ks <1 uM). No numeric specific uptake rates given for this condition. Microaerophilic (0.5-1% O2).

growth_records.medium: defined acidic mineral medium (pH 2.7), nitrate N source
growth_records.cite: Mohammadi et al. Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV, a thermoacidophilic 'Knallgas' methanotroph with both an oxygen-sensitive and -insensitive hydrogenase. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.171. [PMC5364354]
growth_records.snippet: The continuous culture with CH4 as an electron donor and nitrate (NO3-) as N-source had a liquid volume of 550 ml, and was operated at 55 C with stirring at 900 rpm ... supplied with medium at a flow rate of 14.5 ml h-1 (D=0.026 h-1) ... Su
growth_records.notes: Distinct methane + nitrate chemostat (N source = 5 mM KNO3 instead of ammonium). D=0.026 h-1 confirmed from flow 14.5 ml/h / 550 ml = 0.0264 h-1, so mu=0.026; td=ln2/0.026=26.66 h. pH regulated at 2.7 with 1 M bicarbonate. No specific substrate uptake rates reported for this particular reactor. Microaerophilic (8% O2 in gas, controlled).

growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium, H2-limited (nitrate N-source)

up.method: calculated from whole-cell Vmax(app)=340 nmol H2 min-1 mg DW-1 measured by MIMS (Km(app)=195±10 nM); 340 nmol/min/mgDW ×60 = 20400 nmol/h/mgDW = 20.4 mmol/gDW/h

growth_records.cite: Schmitz et al. The thermoacidophilic methanotroph Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV oxidizes subatmospheric H2 with a high-affinity, membrane-associated [NiFe] hydrogenase. ISME J (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0609-3. [PMC7174314]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum H2 oxidation rate (Vmax(app)) was measured to be 340 nmol H2 min−1 mg DW−1 and the cells have an apparent high affinity (Km(app)) for H2 of 195 ± 10 nM ... the cells used were cultivated in a chemostat under such conditions that
growth_records.notes: Whole-cell maximum specific H2 oxidation (uptake) rate. Unit conversion: qH2 = 340 nmol H2 min-1 mgDW-1 × 60 min/h = 20400 nmol h-1 mgDW-1 = 20.4 µmol h-1 mgDW-1 = 20.4 mmol gDW-1 h-1. Km(app) for H2 = 195±10 nM (whole cells; text also cites 190±10 nM). Cells were H2-limited chemostat biomass (nitrate as N-source medium). Whole-cell kinetics measured at 50 °C (MIMS); growth temperature 55 °C. pH not restated for the 0.5 L reactor (main reactor pH 3.0). No CO2-fixation biomass yield or mu reported. This is a max oxidation capacity (Vmax), representing the H2 specific uptake ceiling, not necessarily the steady-state chemostat qH2.

growth_records.medium: mineral medium (Picone et al. 2020) under methanol limitation

up.method: derived from methanethiol co-feed being ~1% of methanol consumption rate
GC-FPD, highest consumption rate 0.21 nmol/min/mgDW

growth_records.cite: Schmitz, Rob A. et al. Methanethiol Consumption and Hydrogen Sulfide Production by the Thermoacidophilic Methanotroph Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.857442. [PMC9003020]
growth_records.snippet: the cells (OD600 ~0.9 or 1.0) grew in a 300 mL chemostat at 55C and pH 2.2 in medium supplemented with 50 mM methanol at a dilution rate D of 0.013 h-1 ... the highest consumption rate of methanethiol was measured at 0.21 nmol.min-1.mg DW-1
growth_records.notes: Methanol-limited chemostat: mu=D=0.013 h-1 at steady state (calculated); doubling time td=ln2/D=0.6931/0.013=53.3 h. Methanethiol co-feed consumption 0.21 nmol/min/mgDW measured by GC; unit conversion 0.21 nmol.min-1.mg-1 = 0.21 umol.min-1.g-1 = 0.21*60/1000 = 0.0126 mmol/gDW/h. Methanol uptake DERIVED: text states methanethiol consumption (0.21 nmol/min/mgDW) is ~1% of methanol consumption, so methanol ~ 0.21/0.01 = 21 nmol/min/mgDW = 21*60/1000 = 1.26 mmol/gDW/h (is_experimental:false, inferred from '~1%'). Full mineral medium composition cited from Picone et al. 2020, not restated here (medium_composition_stated:false; only methanol 50 mM and gas fractions given).

growth_records.medium: mineral medium (Picone et al. 2020); resting methanol-grown cells resuspended for activity assay

up.method: GC-FPD, constant initial-phase rate 0.58 nmol/min/mgDW at ~5 uM start
GC-FPD, up to 1.7 nmol/min/mgDW
Michaelis-Menten fit, Vmax ~2.3 nmol/min/mgDW, Ks ~0.1 uM

sec.units: not quantified (produced during methanethiol oxidation, stoichiometry never 1:1 due to simultaneous H2S consumption; crude extract)

growth_records.cite: Schmitz, Rob A. et al. Methanethiol Consumption and Hydrogen Sulfide Production by the Thermoacidophilic Methanotroph Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.857442. [PMC9003020]
growth_records.snippet: Cells ... incubated with a starting liquid concentration of approximately 5 uM methanethiol consumed this compound at a constant rate of 0.58 nmol.min-1.mg DW-1 ... consumption rates of up to 1.7 nmol.min-1.mg DW-1 were measured. From the d
growth_records.notes: Resting-cell (non-growing) activity assay, so no mu. Measured methanethiol oxidation rates from methanol-chemostat-grown cells. Unit conversions (nmol.min-1.mgDW-1 -> mmol/gDW/h, factor *60/1000 = *0.06): 0.58->0.0348; 1.7->0.102; Vmax 2.3->0.138 mmol/gDW/h. Ks ~0.1 uM. H2S produced during methanethiol oxidation but not quantified as a rate (non-1:1 stoichiometry, simultaneous production+consumption in crude extract, Fig 4B). Cells unable to fully consume methanethiol >~3 uM (inhibition).

growth_records.medium: growth medium (Picone et al. 2020) with methane as substrate

up.units: not quantified as rate (headspace %CH4 depletion tracked, Fig 3B)
up.method: GC headspace

growth_records.cite: Schmitz, Rob A. et al. Methanethiol Consumption and Hydrogen Sulfide Production by the Thermoacidophilic Methanotroph Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.857442. [PMC9003020]
growth_records.snippet: Cells incubated without methanethiol had a growth rate (mu) of 0.060 h-1 and a doubling time (Td) of 11.5 h
growth_records.notes: Batch growth on methane, control condition (no methanethiol). Reported mu=0.060 h-1, Td=11.5 h (consistent: ln2/0.060=11.55 h). pH 2.2 taken from the acidic growth medium used for this thermoacidophile (chemostat medium; not separately restated for batch). Methane consumption tracked via headspace %CH4 (Fig 3B) but not reported as a numeric specific rate.

growth_records.medium: growth medium (Picone et al. 2020) with methane + ~15 uM methanethiol

up.method: GC headspace

up.units: not quantified as specific rate (1200 nmol / ~15 uM depleted after 71 h; microbial + chemical degradation)
up.method: GC-FPD

growth_records.cite: Schmitz, Rob A. et al. Methanethiol Consumption and Hydrogen Sulfide Production by the Thermoacidophilic Methanotroph Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum SolV. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.857442. [PMC9003020]
growth_records.snippet: cells incubated with 15 uM methanethiol (depleted after 71 h) had a mu of 0.043 +/- 0.003 h-1 (Td of 16.4 +/- 1.0 h)
growth_records.notes: Batch growth on methane WITH ~15 uM methanethiol (1200 nmol added). Reported mu=0.043 +/- 0.003 h-1, Td=16.4 +/- 1.0 h (ln2/0.043=16.1 h, consistent). Methanethiol inhibits/prolongs lag; growth rate reduced vs 0.060 h-1 control. Methanethiol depleted after 71 h by combined microbial + chemical degradation (chemical decay yields dimethyldisulfide).

growth_records.medium: Ammonium Mineral Salts (AMS) medium

up.method: experimental (substrate uptake rate, Table 1)
derived from measured CH4:O2 ratio 1:1.62 x qCH4

sec.method: derived from measured CH4:CO2 ratio 1:0.93 x qCH4

growth_records.cite: Saldivar, Alexis et al. Genome-scale flux balance analysis reveals redox trade-offs in the metabolism of the thermoacidophile Methylacidiphilum fumariolicum under auto-, hetero- and methanotrophic conditions. Frontiers in Systems Biology (2024). DOI:10.3389/fsysb.2024.1291612. [PMC12341988]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: strain Pic, CH4, Experimental: qS 3.5 mmol gDW-1 h-1, mu 0.015 h-1, YO2 1.62, YCO2 0.93, YX 0.12. "Growth rates and yields were determined at 50C and pH 2.0. The CH4:O2 ratio was 1:1.6; CH4:CO2 ratio of 1:0.93".
growth_records.notes: Own experimental data of this study (strain Pic isolated from Pichucalco crater lake). qS_CH4 = 3.5 mmol gDW-1 h-1; mu = 0.015 /h (experimental). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.015 = 46.21 h. qO2 computed = YO2 x qS = 1.62 x 3.5 = 5.67 mmol/gDW/h (YO2 = measured CH4:O2 molar ratio 1:1.62). qCO2 computed = YCO2 x qS = 0.93 x 3.5 = 3.255 mmol/gDW/h (measured CH4:CO2 ratio 1:0.93). YX = 0.12 C-mol biomass per mol CH4 (biomass FW assumed 24.6 C-mol gDW-1). Batch, AMS medium pH 2, 50C. Simulation rows for Pic in Table 1 (mu 0.037 CH4, etc.) are FBA predictions and are NOT recorded as experimental.
